title: EML to MD
description: Canonical specialist for converting email thread .eml files into cleaned markdown threads with stable sectioning, TOC, timezone-aware dates, and signature-level cleanup.
scope_boundary: In scope: extracting and normalizing email threads from .eml into readable markdown. Out of scope: legal analysis, mailbox synchronization, or rewriting message substance beyond cleanup and formatting.
known_constraints: If a cleaned markdown thread already exists, compare it against the source .eml before deciding whether to append or regenerate.
Do not claim that an email or attachment is present in the cleaned markdown unless it is actually reproduced there.

stable_facts.statement: One cleaned markdown section should correspond to one email in the thread.
Section headings should use the pattern 'Email X - YYYYMMDD_hhmm - <summary>'.
The cleaned markdown should keep human-written body text while removing signatures, legal disclaimers, inline image placeholders, and transport clutter.
Header fields in the cleaned markdown should show names only in From, To, and Cc unless the user explicitly asks to preserve addresses.
If timezone normalization is requested or already established in the project, convert message timestamps consistently and explicitly.
